question: QatarEnergy announces/resumes LNG production in Qatar by April 30?
description:
QatarEnergy, a Qatari state-owned petroleum company, announced on March 2 that it would halt production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) due to military strikes on its operating facilities (see: https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/MediaCenter/Pages/newsdetails.aspx?ItemId=3892).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if QatarEnergy resumes production of liquefied natural gas at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar, or officially announces that such production has resumed or will resume, by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

QatarEnergy resuming the production of other halted products, including downstream LNG-related products, or resuming transportation of LNG without resuming production, will not alone count.

An official announcement that LNG production will resume at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar must signal the end of the total LNG production halt effective immediately or on a specific date or clearly defined time window. Mere statements that production will resume at some undefined point in the future, or that production will resume once the halt has ended, will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from QatarEnergy (https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/Pages/vHome.aspx); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: QatarEnergy's preparations to restart LNG production at Ras Laffan facilities post a March 2026 halt from Iranian missile strikes drove trader consensus to near-certainty on resumption by April 30, as Reuters reported on April 8 that two of three trains at Qatargas-1 (QELNG North 1) had restarted amid a US-Iran ceasefire enabling safe Strait of Hormuz transits. This partial revival, confirmed by industry sources, aligns with market criteria for "resumes," reflecting the wisdom of crowds where traders wagered over $1.5 million on real-money assessments. Full capacity ramp-up may take months due to damage, but barring renewed military escalation, official confirmation disputes, or procedural resolution challenges via UMA oracle, the outcome appears locked in.

question: LoL: Kiwoom DRX vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Nongshim Red Force in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 2 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran for a permanent peace deal remain stalled despite an extended ceasefire, with President Trump rejecting Tehran's latest proposal on May 1 over unresolved demands including sanctions relief, nuclear curbs, and conditions for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Initial talks in Pakistan collapsed in mid-April amid mutual accusations, but mediators facilitated a fragile truce that halted airstrikes and blockades while preserving leverage for Phase 2 discussions on ballistic missiles and regional security. Deep trust issues, Iran's insistence on no indefinite nuclear limits, and U.S. insistence on verifiable de-escalation signals keep trader consensus skeptical of a breakthrough by June 30, though Pakistan-brokered sessions could resume soon amid economic pressures on both sides.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 97.7% probability against the Iranian regime falling by May 31, driven by its security apparatus's effective suppression of dissent amid the ongoing 2025–2026 protests and US-Israel airstrikes. In the past 30 days, a reported execution spree launched April 1 quelled immediate uprising fears, while no verified mass defections, territorial losses, or opposition breakthroughs have materialized despite economic pressures from Strait of Hormuz disruptions and leadership decapitation strikes killing figures like Ayatollah Khamenei. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) remains intact, anchoring stability. Realistic shifts could stem from uncontainable protests, IRGC infighting, or escalated ground operations, though diplomatic ceasefire signals temper such risks.

Trader consensus reflects a razor-close stylistic clash—volume striker versus one-punch threat—with no reported injuries altering pre-fight dynamics.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing reciprocal blockades by Iran and the United States have kept Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic at about 5% of pre-February 2026 crisis levels, with only 6-14 vessels—mostly Iran-linked—transiting daily versus the normal 125-140, per recent Kpler and Reuters tracking. Despite an April 8 ceasefire, Iran reimposed restrictions after ship attacks and failed negotiations, while US naval enforcement redirects Iran-bound tankers, stranding over 2,000 vessels in the Persian Gulf. Fresh US warnings on May 2 against sanctions for paying Iranian tolls underscore persistent risks, alongside elevated insurance premiums and routing uncertainty. Traders' 94.5% consensus against normalization by May 15 reflects this stalemate, though a diplomatic breakthrough or de-escalation could shift odds.

description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch against Invictus Gaming with momentum from a four-series win streak, including a 2-0 sweep over IG last week and a stunning 2-0 upset of Top Esports, bolstering their 6-3 record and top-four playoff positioning in the double round-robin format. Invictus Gaming, sitting at 4-5 after snapping a skid with a 2-1 win over Weibo Gaming, relies on top laner Breathe and jungler Wei to counter AL's potent top-jungle duo of Tarzan and sasi amid patch 16.08 macro shifts favoring aggressive early game. Head-to-head favors AL recently, but IG's home crowd and desperation for climbing could spark an upset in this pivotal group stage clash.

description:

A Pakistan-brokered ceasefire took effect April 8, extended multiple times despite mutual accusations of violations like US naval blockades of Iranian ports and tanker seizures through late April. Failed Islamabad talks and President Trump's May 1 War Powers notification underscore a strategy of air, naval, and cyber pressure over boots-on-the-ground operations, with Iran's proxies and terrain posing major barriers despite recent national security deliberations on next steps.

question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by May 5, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire following the 2026 US-Iran war, President Trump's rejection on May 1 of Iran's latest proposal—to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for lifting the US blockade—has deepened negotiation stalemates, tempering trader expectations for imminent diplomatic meetings. This comes after late April cancellations of second-round direct talks in Pakistan, despite the landmark in-person high-level session in Islamabad on April 11 that failed to yield a deal on nuclear limits, ballistic missiles, and sanctions. Indirect channels via Oman and Pakistan remain active, but Tehran's denials of near-term direct engagement highlight uncertainty, with potential for resumption hinging on concessions amid escalation risks.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran for a permanent peace deal remain stalled despite an extended ceasefire, with President Trump rejecting Tehran's latest proposal on May 1 over unresolved demands including sanctions relief, nuclear curbs, and conditions for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Initial talks in Pakistan collapsed in mid-April amid mutual accusations, but mediators facilitated a fragile truce that halted airstrikes and blockades while preserving leverage for Phase 2 discussions on ballistic missiles and regional security. Deep trust issues, Iran's insistence on no indefinite nuclear limits, and U.S. insistence on verifiable de-escalation signals keep trader consensus skeptical of a breakthrough by June 30, though Pakistan-brokered sessions could resume soon amid economic pressures on both sides.

question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid stalled US-Iran negotiations following 2025 strikes on Iranian nuclear sites, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi stated on April 29 that most of Iran's roughly 440 kilograms of 60% enriched uranium—enough for multiple warheads if further processed—likely remains buried at the Isfahan complex targeted by US and Israeli airstrikes, with no agency access for verification. The White House reported on April 8 that Iran signaled willingness to hand over the stockpile, but talks falter over US demands for a 20-year enrichment suspension versus Tehran's shorter timeline and assertions that enriched uranium is "sacred." President Trump's insistence that US personnel will secure it underscores tensions; upcoming diplomatic rounds or IAEA inspections could catalyze transfer via deal, seizure, or international oversight before year-end resolution windows.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of May?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely curtailed at about 5% of pre-crisis averages, with only a handful of vessels transiting daily amid mutual US-Iran blockades and attacks on commercial ships since late February. Recent escalations, including Iran's April 30 threats of "long and painful strikes" against renewed US actions and stalled talks in Muscat, have deepened the deadlock, stranding roughly 2,000 ships in the Gulf. While the US is assembling an international coalition for maritime freedom operations, no de-escalation signals have emerged, anchoring trader consensus at an 80.5% implied probability against normalization—defined as sustained 60 daily transits—by May's end despite fluctuating odds in recent weeks.

question: Will a U.S. anti-cartel operation outside of the United States occur by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participate on the ground in an anti-cartel operation or conduct a kinetic strike directed against a cartel on foreign soil by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count.

Only direct U.S. participation, confirmed by the U.S. Government or by an overwhelming consensus of reporting, will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government; however, an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on this Polymarket reflects debate over a April 19, 2026, Chihuahua meth lab raid in Mexico, where two CIA officers died alongside Mexican officials in a post-operation crash, amid questions of direct U.S. ground participation versus advisory roles in drone surveillance and drug handling. Mexican authorities, including special prosecutor Wendy Paola Chávez, emphasized the Americans were unarmed with no operational interaction, falling short of market criteria requiring confirmed kinetic or on-ground involvement by U.S. government personnel. This follows President Trump's March formalization of the 17-nation Americas Counter Cartel Coalition and joint U.S.-Ecuadorian operations targeting narco-terrorists, though those involved U.S. support for local forces. Ongoing Mexican sovereignty probes and 2026 World Cup security pressures for U.S.-Mexico co-hosting could prompt clarifying U.S. statements or escalations influencing resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Shamil Gaziev, ranked in the top 15 heavyweights at 14-2, seeks redemption from a November knockout loss to Waldo Cortes-Acosta that halted his win streak, entering UFC Perth prelims after dropping over 40 pounds to hit 264 lbs at yesterday's weigh-ins. Opposing him is 6-1 Australian knockout specialist Brando "The Balkan Bear" Peričić, who made 265 lbs cleanly and rides two first-round UFC KOs—most recently versus Louie Sutherland in March—on short notice with home-crowd energy at RAC Arena. Peričić's 6'5" frame and 79-inch reach edge favor striking exchanges against Gaziev's grappling base from his debut submission win, amid questions on the Bahrain native's chin durability. No injuries reported, setting up a finish-heavy stylistic clash.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the Iranian regime's demonstrated resilience amid the 2026 Iran war, with U.S.-Israeli airstrikes degrading military capabilities but failing to trigger collapse or mass defections in security forces like the IRGC. Brutal crackdowns crushed the 2025–2026 nationwide protests sparked by economic woes—inflation, unemployment, and internet blackouts—killing thousands and restoring order by February, per reports of Supreme Leader Khamenei's orders to the Supreme National Security Council. Recent April assessments warn of potential unrest from prolonged naval blockades and industrial shutdowns, but no significant escalations have materialized in the past 30 days, solidifying low odds of regime fall by June 30 despite opposition calls and exile rhetoric. Late-breaking diplomatic shifts or internal fractures could still alter this trajectory.

question: Will the Toronto Raptors win the 2026 NBA Finals?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Toronto Raptors win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions after sweeping the Phoenix Suns 4-0 in the Western Conference first round, capped by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 31-42 point performances amid their league-best regular-season mark and elite defense. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 20.0% following a strong No. 2 West seed finish where they nearly overtook OKC late in the year, powered by Victor Wembanyama's breakout and playoff momentum like a 3-1 series lead. Boston Celtics' 9.3% reflects Eastern Conference dominance but faces tougher path against surging West powers; Knicks, Pistons, and Cavaliers trail amid inconsistent East playoff showings and injury concerns as conference semifinals loom.

question: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 25 bps after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's June 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 95.5% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on the Fed's cautious pause amid sticky inflation and resilient labor conditions. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while core PCE hit 3.2%, exceeding targets, and nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs with jobless claims hovering near 190,000. The April 28-29 FOMC held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% for the third meeting, backed by officials like Dallas Fed President Logan signaling the next move could be a cut or hike. Weaker-than-expected April CPI (due May 12) or softening employment data could challenge this positioning by reviving rate-cut odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Steve Erceg enters as the trader-favored striker in his Perth homecoming at UFC Fight Night 275's main card flyweight bout against veteran grappler Tim Elliott, buoyed by a recent unanimous decision win over Ode Osbourne that snapped a two-fight skid against top competition like Alexandre Pantoja and Brandon Moreno. Elliott, riding a multi-win streak including a gritty victory over Kai Asakura despite a broken leg, has overhauled his camp by ditching strength training for pure sparring to sharpen his chaotic wrestling and scramble game. Both made weight—Elliott at 126 lbs, Erceg at 125.5—with no reported injuries; Erceg's reach advantage and takedown defense temper Elliott's pressure, while crowd energy could fuel the local contender's volume striking in this stylistic clash.

This skin-in-the-game assessment reflects no viable path to normalization by deadline, though tail risks like an abrupt U.S.-Iran ceasefire or rapid de-escalation could theoretically prompt a final-hour data revision before resolution.

question: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by May 14, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be Chair of the Federal Reserve for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Jerome Powell's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Jerome Powell out as Fed Chair by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jerome Powell ceases to be the Chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve for any period of time between this market's creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, 2026, after which he confirmed on April 29 he will step down from the role but remain a Board governor until 2028, citing legal protections amid administration pressures. President Trump nominated former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh as successor, who cleared the Senate Banking Committee the same day, setting up a full Senate confirmation vote that could enable a swift transition. This unusual decision to stay on the Board breaks historical norms where chairs typically depart entirely, fueling debate over Fed independence and policy continuity. Traders focus on Senate timeline and potential holds, with upcoming confirmation hearings as the key catalyst amid ongoing tensions over interest rates and inflation management.

question: Will China invade Taiwan by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China including any inhabited islands will qualify, however uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will China invade Taiwan by end of 2026?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: US intelligence's March 2026 Annual Threat Assessment concluded Chinese leaders lack plans for a Taiwan invasion, even by 2027, with no fixed timeline amid ongoing People's Liberation Army purges that have decimated command structures for complex amphibious operations. Routine Chinese military drills in the East China Sea and around Taiwan in mid-April, described by Beijing as justified responses to perceived provocations, have not signaled escalation to full invasion preparations. Taiwan's fortified defenses, bolstered asymmetric capabilities, and strengthened US alliances further deter action, while China's economic challenges amplify invasion costs. Traders' 92.5% "No" consensus reflects these de-escalation signals and high barriers, though late-breaking diplomatic ruptures or military surges could shift odds before year-end.

question: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Anyone's Legend - Game 1 Winner
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win Game 1 against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win Game 1 against Invictus Gaming.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 2 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Invictus Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 rematch against Invictus Gaming with momentum from a four-series win streak, including a 2-0 sweep over IG last week and a stunning 2-0 upset of Top Esports, bolstering their 6-3 record and top-four playoff positioning in the double round-robin format. Invictus Gaming, sitting at 4-5 after snapping a skid with a 2-1 win over Weibo Gaming, relies on top laner Breathe and jungler Wei to counter AL's potent top-jungle duo of Tarzan and sasi amid patch 16.08 macro shifts favoring aggressive early game. Head-to-head favors AL recently, but IG's home crowd and desperation for climbing could spark an upset in this pivotal group stage clash.

question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US military strikes targeted over 90 Iranian installations on Kharg Island, Iran's critical oil export hub handling 90% of its shipments, on April 7 amid escalating tensions in the ongoing Iran conflict and disputes over Strait of Hormuz control. Iranian state media asserts the island remains fully secured by its armed forces, with oil flows uninterrupted despite US naval blockades filling storage tanks and threatening shutdowns. President Trump's public suggestions of potential ground seizure by US Marines to leverage economic pressure persist, but no occupation has occurred. Traders weigh de-escalation signals against further airstrikes or invasion risks ahead of the May 31 resolution, reflecting uncertainty in this proxy escalation hotspot.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by May 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by May 31 at just 6%, reflecting stalled US-mediated negotiations and maximalist territorial demands from both Moscow and Kyiv despite recent diplomatic feelers. Russian President Putin proposed a short-term Victory Day truce on May 9 via talks with US President Trump, prompting Ukraine to seek clarification through Washington, but prior Easter pauses and February talks ended without breakthroughs amid mutual accusations of violations. Intensified Ukrainian long-range strikes on Russian oil refineries and faltering Russian offensives in Donetsk sustain frontline escalation, with no scheduled summits or concessions signaling de-escalation before late May. Late-breaking diplomacy or external pressures could shift odds, though historical patterns favor prolonged stalemate.

question: Will Donald Trump announce that the United States blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has been lifted by May 15, 2026?
description:
On April 12, 2026, President Donald Trump announced that the United States will blockade the Strait of Hormuz. You can read more about that here: https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-us-iran-fail-reach-deal-peace-talks-day-negotiations-rcna315918.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the US military publicly and officially announces the end of the United States blockade of the Strait of Hormuz by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Qualifying statements must clearly and explicitly indicate that the United States has lifted, ended, or will lift or end its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on a specified date or use equivalently definitive language unambiguously signaling that such blockade has ceased or is set to cease on a specified date (e.g., statements unambiguously indicating that US naval activity in the relevant area has ceased will qualify). 

Statements that merely describe actions inconsistent with the blockade (e.g., "Iran resumed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz") without explicitly indicating the blockade as lifted will not alone suffice.

Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources, or leaks do not qualify.

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g., posts from his personal Truth Social account) will qualify. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Note: this market will resolve solely based on whether a qualifying announcement is made within the specified timeframe. Whether the blockade is effectively enforced or whether maritime traffic resumes absent a qualifying announcement will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump imposed a US naval blockade on Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz on April 12 amid the ongoing Iran war now exceeding 60 days, aiming to force Tehran to reopen the vital shipping lane; Iran briefly lifted its restrictions on April 17-18 before reimposing them, demanding the US action end first. Recent rejections of Iranian proposals by Trump on April 29, coupled with US efforts to form an international maritime coalition on April 30 and sanctions warnings to shipping firms on May 2, underscore an impasse with no announcement to lift the blockade. Traders weigh stalled diplomacy, potential escalation signals, and economic pressures from surging oil prices against prospects for a deal, with naval operations continuing unabated.

question: Counter-Strike: Natus Vincere vs FaZe (BO3) - BLAST Rivals Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 1 match between Natus Vincere and FaZe in the BLAST Rivals Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 2 at 1:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against FaZe.

This market will resolve to "FaZe" if FaZe win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Natus Vincere, ranked #2 on HLTV, holds a strong edge heading into this BO3 semi-final rematch against #17 FaZe at BLAST Rivals 2026 Season 1 playoffs in Fort Worth, following a dominant 2-0 group stage win on Anubis (13-7) and Ancient (13-11), where makazze earned player of the match honors with a 1.46 rating. FaZe, sporting a new-look roster without karrigan and featuring frozen, Twistzz, broky, jcobbb, and Neityu, has built momentum through upsets, including a 2-1 quarterfinal victory over higher-ranked G2 via clutches on Mirage overtime after dropping Dust2. NaVi's stable lineup of Aleksib, iM, b1t, w0nderful, and makazze boasts a 66.7% win rate over the last three months, contrasting FaZe's 41.7%, with map vetoes and individual form like w0nderful's consistency key factors in this single-elimination clash toward the $1M prize pool.

question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $80,000 on May 2?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Los Angeles Lakers win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 55.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions after sweeping the Phoenix Suns 4-0 in the Western Conference first round, capped by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 31-42 point performances amid their league-best regular-season mark and elite defense. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 20.0% following a strong No. 2 West seed finish where they nearly overtook OKC late in the year, powered by Victor Wembanyama's breakout and playoff momentum like a 3-1 series lead. Boston Celtics' 9.3% reflects Eastern Conference dominance but faces tougher path against surging West powers; Knicks, Pistons, and Cavaliers trail amid inconsistent East playoff showings and injury concerns as conference semifinals loom.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran for a permanent peace deal remain stalled despite an extended ceasefire, with President Trump rejecting Tehran's latest proposal on May 1 over unresolved demands including sanctions relief, nuclear curbs, and conditions for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Initial talks in Pakistan collapsed in mid-April amid mutual accusations, but mediators facilitated a fragile truce that halted airstrikes and blockades while preserving leverage for Phase 2 discussions on ballistic missiles and regional security. Deep trust issues, Iran's insistence on no indefinite nuclear limits, and U.S. insistence on verifiable de-escalation signals keep trader consensus skeptical of a breakthrough by June 30, though Pakistan-brokered sessions could resume soon amid economic pressures on both sides.

question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from AtlasIntel/Bloomberg, Datafolha, and Nexus/BTG Pactual, conducted in late April 2026, depict a razor-thin first-round contest between incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), with Flávio leading or tying in multiple surveys—mirroring Polymarket's trader consensus favoring Flávio at 43.5% implied probability over Lula's 37.5%. This tightness stems from enduring polarization: Lula's loyal leftist base and incumbency advantages clashing against Flávio's surge as the right-wing frontrunner, consolidating anti-PT sentiment amid former President Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility. Fragmented fields favor a runoff, but separation could arise from economic data releases, party conventions formalizing candidacies by June, endorsements, scandals, or regional governor races influencing national turnout dynamics ahead of the October 4 first round.

question: Will Elon Musk post 90-114 tweets from April 30 to May 2, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 30 12:00 PM ET to May 2, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Absent major catalysts like Tesla announcements, SpaceX launches, or political flare-ups, this moderate volume aligns with his baseline cultural engagement amid ongoing business focus. Upsets remain possible via a sudden viral controversy sparking a 50+ post spree—historical precedents show 100+ daily bursts—or uncharacteristically low activity if consumed by executive demands, though current trajectory locks in the frontrunner. Market closes end of May 2.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and June 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for June 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 54.5% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz transit calls reaching a 7-day moving average of 60 or higher by June 30, driven by persistent US-Iran standoffs that have suppressed daily vessel flows to 10-14 versus pre-crisis norms of 60+, per live trackers and IMF Portwatch data through late April averaging under 12. Recent failed Islamabad talks and a reversed April ceasefire, coupled with ongoing US naval blockades turning back Iranian tankers and IRGC-imposed restrictions, maintain uncertainty despite the May 1 US-led Maritime Freedom Construct. Elevated war-risk premiums at 1% and VLCC spot rates up 460% deter transits, while Brent crude hovers near $95 amid supply fears. Key swing factors include diplomatic progress or escalation, with upcoming IMF Portwatch updates and EIA inventories pivotal for resolution shifts.

question: Will the Fed increase interest rates by 25 bps after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's June 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 95.5% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on the Fed's cautious pause amid sticky inflation and resilient labor conditions. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while core PCE hit 3.2%, exceeding targets, and nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs with jobless claims hovering near 190,000. The April 28-29 FOMC held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% for the third meeting, backed by officials like Dallas Fed President Logan signaling the next move could be a cut or hike. question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $200 in May?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point after market creation during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours. 

Per CME contract specifications for WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, a contract’s last trading day is three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day).

The active month changes at the start of the second trading session prior to the nearest listed contract’s last trading session. At that point, the next listed contract becomes the active month (i.e., for the final three trading sessions of the nearest listed contract, the contract for the next month is the active month). The trading session for a given business day typically begins at 6:00 PM ET on the prior calendar date.

For example, if the 25th of the month is a Saturday, the last trading session for the nearest listed contract is the session for Tuesday the 21st, and the next listed contract becomes the active month at the start of the trading session for Friday the 17th (6:00 PM ET on Thursday), assuming a standard trading calendar.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's June 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

question: Will Elon Musk post 80-99 tweets from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 28 12:00 PM ET to May 5, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $150 in May?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point after market creation during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours. 

Per CME contract specifications for WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, a contract’s last trading day is three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day).

The active month changes at the start of the second trading session prior to the nearest listed contract’s last trading session. At that point, the next listed contract becomes the active month (i.e., for the final three trading sessions of the nearest listed contract, the contract for the next month is the active month). The trading session for a given business day typically begins at 6:00 PM ET on the prior calendar date.

For example, if the 25th of the month is a Saturday, the last trading session for the nearest listed contract is the session for Tuesday the 21st, and the next listed contract becomes the active month at the start of the trading session for Friday the 17th (6:00 PM ET on Thursday), assuming a standard trading calendar.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

question: Will the Fed increase interest rates by 50+ bps after the June 2026 meeting?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's June 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for June 16-17, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 95.5% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on the Fed's cautious pause amid sticky inflation and resilient labor conditions. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—while core PCE hit 3.2%, exceeding targets, and nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs with jobless claims hovering near 190,000. The April 28-29 FOMC held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% for the third meeting, backed by officials like Dallas Fed President Logan signaling the next move could be a cut or hike. Weaker-than-expected April CPI (due May 12) or softening employment data could challenge this positioning by reviving rate-cut odds.

Marseille, sixth with 51 points chasing European spots, drew 1-1 at Nice last time but welcome back Mason Greenwood despite Timothy Weah's suspension and absences like Nayef Aguerd and Geoffrey Kondogbia; their unbeaten streak in the prior six visits to Stade de la Beaujoire underpins the narrow edge in this survival-vs-Europe clash.

question: Will Donald Trump announce that the United States blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has been lifted by May 31, 2026?
description:
On April 12, 2026, President Donald Trump announced that the United States will blockade the Strait of Hormuz. You can read more about that here: https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/live-blog/live-updates-us-iran-fail-reach-deal-peace-talks-day-negotiations-rcna315918.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the US military publicly and officially announces the end of the United States blockade of the Strait of Hormuz by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Qualifying statements must clearly and explicitly indicate that the United States has lifted, ended, or will lift or end its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on a specified date or use equivalently definitive language unambiguously signaling that such blockade has ceased or is set to cease on a specified date (e.g., statements unambiguously indicating that US naval activity in the relevant area has ceased will qualify). 

Statements that merely describe actions inconsistent with the blockade (e.g., "Iran resumed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz") without explicitly indicating the blockade as lifted will not alone suffice.

Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources, or leaks do not qualify.

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g., posts from his personal Truth Social account) will qualify. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Note: this market will resolve solely based on whether a qualifying announcement is made within the specified timeframe. Whether the blockade is effectively enforced or whether maritime traffic resumes absent a qualifying announcement will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump imposed a US naval blockade on Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz on April 12 amid the ongoing Iran war now exceeding 60 days, aiming to force Tehran to reopen the vital shipping lane; Iran briefly lifted its restrictions on April 17-18 before reimposing them, demanding the US action end first. Recent rejections of Iranian proposals by Trump on April 29, coupled with US efforts to form an international maritime coalition on April 30 and sanctions warnings to shipping firms on May 2, underscore an impasse with no announcement to lift the blockade. Traders weigh stalled diplomacy, potential escalation signals, and economic pressures from surging oil prices against prospects for a deal, with naval operations continuing unabated.

question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
description:

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: La Bisbal: Tamara Korpatsch vs Marina Bassols Ribera
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tamara Korpatsch and Marina Bassols Ribera in the La Bisbal, originally scheduled for May 2, 2026 at 6:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Tamara Korpatsch' if Tamara Korpatsch advances against Marina Bassols Ribera.

question: Will a new MAI model be released by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Microsoft releases any new MAI model that is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A “new MAI model” refers to any model explicitly designated by Microsoft as part of its MAI (Microsoft AI) model family, including new base models or successors within that family.

Any releases of task-specialized models, cost-efficiency variants, or similar variants will count for this market.

Products labeled as outside of the MAI family will NOT qualify.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," a qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice.

The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by Microsoft as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Microsoft (microsoft.ai), with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by May 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.
